.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:34335:### Finding 1: Mnemosyne does not create a first-class session; it creates a collection context
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:34340:- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:34343:### Finding 2: Mnemosyne session maintenance is agent-discipline, not runtime state
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:34348:- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:34351:### Finding 3: Mnemosyne has no stop-hook summarization or autosave path

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:34372:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:34375:### Finding 6: Mnemosyne’s lifecycle hygiene is document deletion, not session closure
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:34380:- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:40004:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:40005:### Finding 1: Mnemosyne’s wrapper exposes a search contract, not a visible ranking engine

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:51092:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:51095:### Finding 2: Mnemosyne has no passive capture path; it is manual-store plus reminder-driven behavior
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:51099:- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:51102:### Finding 3: Multi-agent safety is effectively absent at the plugin boundary

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:51120:- **Recommendation**: adopt now

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:51127:- **Recommendation**: reject

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:55733:### Finding 1: Mnemosyne’s recall surface throws away retrieval structure that Public already uses
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:55737:- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:55740:### Finding 2: The wrapper intentionally makes hybrid retrieval opaque by collapsing the query into one quoted backend string
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:55744:- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:55747:### Finding 3: Public’s `retrievalLevel` is a richer retrieval control than Mnemosyne’s separate project/global recall tools
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:55751:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:55754:### Finding 4: Mnemosyne persists memories across sessions, but it does not expose session-aware retrieval continuity
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:55758:- **Recommendation**: adopt now

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:55772:- **Recommendation**: reject

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:60483:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:60484:### Finding 1: Mnemosyne bootstraps storage, not sessions
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:60488:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:60491:### Finding 2: Mnemosyne is stateless at the tool boundary; Public mints and defends session identity
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:60495:- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:60498:### Finding 3: Mnemosyne survives compaction with reminders; Public resumes with actual session state
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:60502:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:60505:### Finding 4: Mnemosyne’s end-of-session lifecycle is manual note taking; `generate-context.js` is structured session serialization
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:60509:- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:60512:### Finding 5: Mnemosyne ties memory identity to directory basename, which makes lifecycle continuity fragile across renames and parallel checkouts

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:60694:Research OpenCode Mnemosyne's hybrid search architecture, plugin design patterns, and compaction survival strategies to identify practical, evidence-backed improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially around combining text and vector search, OpenCode plugin integration, project-vs-global memory scoping, and preserving memory awareness across context compaction. Classify each recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:60741:15. Save all outputs inside this phase folder, especially under `research/`. Every meaningful finding must cite exact file paths, say whether the evidence is wrapper-confirmed or README-level, explain why it matters for `Code_Environment/Public`, classify the recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`, and identify the affected subsystem. When research is complete, update `checklist.md`, create `implementation-summary.md`, and save memory with:

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:66779:### Finding 1: Mnemosyne’s hygiene contract lives in prose and prompt injection, not enforceable tooling
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:66783:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:66786:### Finding 2: Mnemosyne stores opaque memory snippets; Public’s save path scores, dedups, and enriches memory quality
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:66790:- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:66793:### Finding 3: Mnemosyne has no trigger system, so retrieval hygiene stays agent-driven instead of signal-driven
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:66797:- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:66800:### Finding 4: Mnemosyne’s mutation hygiene is single-shot delete/manual overwrite; Public mutations are transactional and auditable
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:66804:- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:66807:### Finding 5: Mnemosyne hides readiness drift behind auto-init; Public makes hygiene failures observable and repairable
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:66811:- **Recommendation**: prototype later

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:66818:- **Recommendation**: reject

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98425:### Finding 1: The strongest pattern is compaction-time preservation of memory behavior, not memory content
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98430:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98433:### Finding 2: Mnemosyne turns memory use into a lifecycle contract by repeating the same policy at startup and compaction
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98438:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98441:### Finding 3: The hook compresses scope policy into a few agent-facing verbs, which makes it usable under pressure
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98446:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98449:### Finding 4: Public already preserves continuity across compaction, but its OpenCode surface intentionally avoids owning retrieval policy
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98454:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98457:### Finding 5: We can borrow the strongest pattern without borrowing Mnemosyne’s backend architecture
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:98462:- **Recommendation**: adopt now

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:102967:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:102968:### Finding 1: Mnemosyne’s hybrid story stays inside one memory plane, while Public already codifies a federated retrieval stack
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:102972:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:102975:### Finding 2: Public’s memory plane already goes deeper than Mnemosyne’s project/global split through `retrievalLevel` and community fallback
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:102979:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:102982:### Finding 3: Public operationalizes retrieval federation at runtime; Mnemosyne mostly documents it as expected agent behavior
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:102986:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:102989:### Finding 4: Mnemosyne’s project/global model is a thin UX alias over collection naming, not a replacement for Public’s governed scope model
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:102993:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:102996:### Finding 5: Public has a contract-visibility gap: `retrievalLevel` exists in runtime behavior but not in the shared `memory_search` schema
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:103000:- **Recommendation**: adopt now

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:125594:Research OpenCode Mnemosyne's hybrid search architecture, plugin design patterns, and compaction survival strategies to identify practical, evidence-backed improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially around combining text and vector search, OpenCode plugin integration, project-vs-global memory scoping, and preserving memory awareness across context compaction. .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131037:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131038:### Finding 1: Add a transport-owned memory reminder block at compaction time
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131042:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131045:### Finding 2: Mnemosyne’s direct store/delete loop is incompatible with Public’s governed, file-backed mutation model
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131049:- **Recommendation**: adopt now, but only as wording and workflow discipline, not as API semantics
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131052:### Finding 3: Read-side `project/global` aliases are worth prototyping, but only as thin wrappers over existing retrieval scope
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131056:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131059:### Finding 4: Mnemosyne’s AGENTS/startup discipline is reusable only after translating it into Public’s recovery authority
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131063:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131066:### Finding 5: A direct `core=true` shortcut should be rejected on Public’s primary surface
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131070:- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131073:### Finding 6: If we add an OpenCode-native memory facade, it must be delegation-only
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131077:- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE, but only as a delegation-only wrapper
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131080:### Finding 7: Mnemosyne’s offline-first binary/model stack is useful as an optional pattern, not as Public’s default deployment model
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131084:- **Recommendation**: reject as the default adoption path
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131111:- Questions answered: Phase A adopt now: add a deduped memory-tool reminder to the existing compaction transport, and rewrite startup/AGENTS wording around `session_bootstrap` plus governed save flows. Phase B prototype later: add read-only `project/global` recall aliases that compile down to current retrieval scope, with no write aliases. Phase C hold or reject: do not add a direct `core=true` shortcut, do not replace Public’s retrieval stack with Mnemosyne, and only pursue an OpenCode-native facade if it delegates to MCP instead of becoming a second authority.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131131:## Findings

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131205:- Questions answered: Phase A adopt now: add a deduped memory-tool reminder to the existing compaction transport, and rewrite startup/AGENTS wording around `session_bootstrap` plus governed save flows. Phase B prototype later: add read-only `project/global` recall aliases that compile down to current retrieval scope, with no write aliases. Phase C hold or reject: do not add a direct `core=true` shortcut, do not replace Public’s retrieval stack with Mnemosyne, and only pursue an OpenCode-native facade if it delegates to MCP instead of becoming a second authority.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131219:### Finding 5: A direct `core=true` shortcut should be rejected on Public’s primary surface
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131223:- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131226:### Finding 6: If we add an OpenCode-native memory facade, it must be delegation-only
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131230:- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE, but only as a delegation-only wrapper
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131233:### Finding 7: Mnemosyne’s offline-first binary/model stack is useful as an optional pattern, not as Public’s default deployment model
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131237:- **Recommendation**: reject as the default adoption path
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131264:- Questions answered: Phase A adopt now: add a deduped memory-tool reminder to the existing compaction transport, and rewrite startup/AGENTS wording around `session_bootstrap` plus governed save flows. Phase B prototype later: add read-only `project/global` recall aliases that compile down to current retrieval scope, with no write aliases. Phase C hold or reject: do not add a direct `core=true` shortcut, do not replace Public’s retrieval stack with Mnemosyne, and only pursue an OpenCode-native facade if it delegates to MCP instead of becoming a second authority.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131285:Research OpenCode Mnemosyne's hybrid search architecture, plugin design patterns, and compaction survival strategies to identify practical, evidence-backed improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially around combining text and vector search, OpenCode plugin integration, project-vs-global memory scoping, and preserving memory awareness across context compaction. Focus on what the external repo actually proves in `src/index.ts` and what the README describes about the Mnemosyne backend. Classify each recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:131332:15. Save all outputs inside this phase folder, especially under `research/`. Every meaningful finding must cite exact file paths, say whether the evidence is wrapper-confirmed or README-level, explain why it matters for `Code_Environment/Public`, classify the recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`, and identify the affected subsystem. When research is complete, update `checklist.md`, create `implementation-summary.md`, and save memory with:

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135526:## Findings
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135527:### Finding 1: Mnemosyne is not a retrieval-engine upgrade for Public
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135531:- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135534:### Finding 2: Compaction-time memory action guidance is the strongest adopt-now idea
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135538:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135541:### Finding 3: Mnemosyne’s startup/AGENTS discipline should be reused only after translation into Public terms
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135545:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135548:### Finding 4: Read-only `project` / `global` recall aliases are worth prototyping

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135555:### Finding 5: Basename-derived project identity is too weak for Public’s memory authority
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135559:- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135562:### Finding 6: An ergonomic inline memory intake is valid only as a governed facade
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135566:- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135569:### Finding 7: A direct `core=true` shortcut should not be added to Public’s main surface
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135573:- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135576:### Finding 8: Mnemosyne’s blind append and hard-delete lifecycle is the wrong primary mutation model for Public
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135580:- **Recommendation**: reject
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135583:### Finding 9: A native OpenCode memory plugin is viable only as a delegation-only facade
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135587:- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135590:### Finding 10: Optional offline backend readiness is worth prototyping, but Mnemosyne’s runtime stack should not become Public’s default
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135594:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135613:- Executive summary: 10 consolidated decisions. `adopt now`: 2. `prototype later`: 2. `NEW FEATURE`: 2. `reject`: 4. The highest-confidence transfers are compaction-time memory action guidance and rewritten startup policy; the highest-confidence rejections are basename-derived scope, `core=true`, and Mnemosyne-style mutation semantics.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135634:FINAL SYNTHESIS: I consolidated the repeated iteration themes into 10 non-overlapping decisions so every substantive Mnemosyne finding lands in one bucket: `adopt now`, `prototype later`, `NEW FEATURE`, or `reject`.

# Iteration 011: GAP ANALYSIS - MISSING FEATURES

## Focus
GAP ANALYSIS - MISSING FEATURES: What entirely NEW features should we build that we haven't considered?

## Findings
### Finding 1: Add a compaction-time memory action card, not just a continuity note
- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts#L208](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L208), [opencode-transport.ts#L140](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/context/opencode-transport.ts#L140), [spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js#L396](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js#L396)
- **What it does**: Wrapper-confirmed: Mnemosyne pushes an explicit post-compaction checklist into `output.context` telling the agent when to recall, store, delete, use `core`, and use global memory. Public’s transport/plugin path pushes a deduped continuity block, but that block is transport-only state, not a memory-operations playbook.
- **Why it matters for us**: Public preserves state across compaction, but it does not re-teach memory behavior at the moment the agent most needs it. A new compact “memory action card” would complement `session_bootstrap` and structural transport instead of replacing them.
- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 2: Build first-class project and personal memory lanes on top of governance scopes
- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts#L27](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L27), [external/src/index.ts#L78](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L78), [external/src/index.ts#L96](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L96), [external/README.md#L51](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/README.md#L51), [tool-schemas.ts#L42](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts#L42), [tool-schemas.ts#L69](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts#L69), [scope-governance.ts#L21](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/governance/scope-governance.ts#L21), [memory-search.ts#L213](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts#L213)
- **What it does**: Both: Mnemosyne exposes separate project and global tools, with auto-init for project memory and lazy-init for global memory. Public exposes one generalized memory surface with `specFolder`, `tenantId`, `userId`, `agentId`, and `sharedSpaceId`; meanwhile its `retrievalLevel: global` is community fallback, not a personal/global preference lane.
- **Why it matters for us**: Public has the capability, but not the ergonomic product surface. A thin facade like `scope: "project|personal|shared"` or dedicated helper tools would make cross-project preference capture much easier. If we build this, it should use stable workspace identities, not Mnemosyne’s basename-derived namespace, which risks collisions.
- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 3: Add an inline `memory_remember` intake path instead of requiring file-first saves
- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts#L138](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L138), [external/src/index.ts#L160](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L160), [tool-schemas.ts#L217](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts#L217), [memory-save.ts#L1](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-save.ts#L1)
- **What it does**: Wrapper-confirmed versus current Public surface: Mnemosyne stores a concise memory directly from a string argument. Public’s write path is file-centric: `memory_save` accepts an absolute `filePath`, then runs parsing, quality gates, governance, dedup, and embedding.
- **Why it matters for us**: This is a genuine missing feature, not just wrapper thinness. We should add an audited inline intake tool that accepts short text plus minimal metadata, materializes canonical markdown internally, and then runs the existing save pipeline. That would preserve Public’s safety while removing the current authoring friction.
- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 4: Add write-time pinning for always-on memories
- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts#L140](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L140), [external/src/index.ts#L162](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L162), [external/README.md#L55](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/README.md#L55), [tool-schemas.ts#L49](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts#L49), [tool-schemas.ts#L217](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts#L217), [tool-schemas.ts#L291](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts#L291)
- **What it does**: Both: Mnemosyne lets an agent set `core=true` at creation time and treats that as always-relevant context. Public guarantees constitutional memories are always surfaced, but creation-time pinning is not a first-class tool parameter; it is encoded via file metadata or later `memory_update`.
- **Why it matters for us**: Public has the semantics, but not the authoring shortcut. A new `pin: "always"` or `priority: "core"` write-time option would reduce friction for durable user preferences and architecture rules while still mapping into existing constitutional or critical tiers.
- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
- **Impact**: medium

### Finding 5: Build an explicit offline warmup and doctor flow for memory readiness
- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts#L64](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L64), [external/README.md#L7](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/README.md#L7), [external/README.md#L82](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/README.md#L82), [external/package.json#L1](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/package.json#L1), [tool-schemas.ts#L236](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts#L236), [memory-crud-health.ts#L380](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts#L380), [context-server.ts#L1238](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts#L1238), [context-server.ts#L1580](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts#L1580)
- **What it does**: Both: Mnemosyne documents a binary prerequisite and one-time local model download, and its wrapper returns a clear missing-binary install message. Public already has `memory_health` and startup readiness logs, but it still relies on lazy first-use initialization and manual follow-up when embeddings are not ready.
- **Why it matters for us**: Public is operationally richer, but it lacks a proactive “make memory ready now” surface. A `memory_warmup` or `memory_doctor` feature that preloads providers, validates embedding profile, primes indices, and returns deterministic ready/not-ready output would remove a lot of first-query uncertainty.
- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
- **Impact**: medium

## Sources Consulted
- [external/src/index.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts)
- [external/package.json](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/package.json)

- [memory-search.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts)
- [memory-save.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-save.ts)
- [memory-crud-health.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts)
- [context-server.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts)
- [opencode-transport.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/context/opencode-transport.ts)

## Assessment
- New information ratio: 0.79
- Questions addressed: compaction-specific memory guidance, project-vs-global ergonomics, direct memory capture, write-time pinning, offline readiness, AGENTS guidance location, overlap with existing hybrid retrieval and community-search fallback.
- Questions answered: Public already owns the heavy hybrid retrieval, trigger recall, health, and governance story. The real missing surface area is ergonomic: a compaction memory action card, explicit scoped lanes, inline capture, write-time pinning, and proactive warmup. Mnemosyne’s most transferable value is its thin agent-facing contract, not its ranking backend. The exact strict validation command passed with `Summary: Errors: 0  Warnings: 0` and `RESULT: PASSED`, while also emitting the read-only warning `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`. No `AGENTS.md` file was present under the external checkout; that guidance exists only in the README.

## Reflection
- What worked: Reading [external/src/index.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts) first, then tracing directly into Public’s [tool-schemas.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts), [opencode-transport.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/context/opencode-transport.ts), [memory-search.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts), and [memory-crud-health.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts) made the true gaps look like product-surface gaps rather than retrieval-engine gaps.
- What did not work: The sandbox is read-only, so I could not update `research/research.md`, `checklist.md`, `implementation-summary.md`, or save memory for the phase. The external repo also does not expose Mnemosyne’s Go internals, so anything below the wrapper remains README-level contract rather than source-proven implementation.

## Recommended Next Focus
Prototype a thin Public-side memory facade with three pieces together: `memory_remember` for inline capture, a `scope`/`pin` authoring contract for project-personal-always-on memories, and a compaction memory action card injected alongside the existing transport payload. That will test whether Mnemosyne’s best idea for us is not new ranking math, but a much thinner agent-facing memory UX.

- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts#L64](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L64), [external/README.md#L7](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/README.md#L7), [external/README.md#L82](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/README.md#L82), [external/package.json#L1](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/package.json#L1), [tool-schemas.ts#L236](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts#L236), [memory-crud-health.ts#L380](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts#L380), [context-server.ts#L1238](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts#L1238), [context-server.ts#L1580](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts#L1580)
- **What it does**: Both: Mnemosyne documents a binary prerequisite and one-time local model download, and its wrapper returns a clear missing-binary install message. Public already has `memory_health` and startup readiness logs, but it still relies on lazy first-use initialization and manual follow-up when embeddings are not ready.
- **Why it matters for us**: Public is operationally richer, but it lacks a proactive “make memory ready now” surface. A `memory_warmup` or `memory_doctor` feature that preloads providers, validates embedding profile, primes indices, and returns deterministic ready/not-ready output would remove a lot of first-query uncertainty.
- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
- **Impact**: medium

### Finding 2: Compaction-time memory action guidance is the strongest adopt-now idea
- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts:210](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L210), [spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js:396](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js#L396), [opencode-transport.ts:142](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/context/opencode-transport.ts#L142), [memory-surface.ts:640](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/hooks/memory-surface.ts#L640); evidence: both
- **What it does**: Mnemosyne preserves memory behavior across compaction with an explicit action card, while Public currently preserves continuity/state but not equally direct memory-tool verbs on the OpenCode surface.
- **Why it matters for us**: A transport-owned, deduped memory reminder would improve post-compaction behavior without changing retrieval authority.
- **Recommendation**: adopt now
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 3: Mnemosyne’s startup/AGENTS discipline should be reused only after translation into Public terms
- **Source**: [external/README.md:64](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/README.md#L64), [context-server.ts:755](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts#L755), [session-bootstrap.ts:163](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-bootstrap.ts#L163), [memory-save.ts:1136](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-save.ts#L1136); evidence: both
- **What it does**: Mnemosyne repeats “recall early, save after decisions, delete contradictions” in AGENTS and compaction, but Public’s save/delete paths are governed and more constrained.
- **Why it matters for us**: The behavioral pattern is good, but the wording must point to `session_bootstrap`, `memory_context`, governed saves, and careful deletions instead of cheap direct CLI mutations.
- **Recommendation**: adopt now
- **Impact**: medium

### Finding 4: Read-only `project` / `global` recall aliases are worth prototyping
- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts:118](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L118), [external/src/index.ts:160](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L160), [memory-search.ts:529](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts#L529), [memory-search.ts:818](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts#L818); evidence: wrapper-confirmed
- **What it does**: Mnemosyne gives simple project/global tool pairs; Public already has richer runtime scoping via `retrievalLevel` and broader fallback behavior.
- **Why it matters for us**: Thin read aliases could improve ergonomics, but only if they compile down to existing scope logic and do not invent new authority.
- **Recommendation**: prototype later
- **Impact**: medium

### Finding 5: Basename-derived project identity is too weak for Public’s memory authority
- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts:28](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L28), [external/src/index.ts:79](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L79), [external/README.md:55](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/README.md#L55), [memory-save.ts:1265](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-save.ts#L1265); evidence: both
- **What it does**: Mnemosyne normalizes the current directory basename into a collection key and auto-inits that collection on load.
- **Why it matters for us**: That is fragile across renames, sibling worktrees, and same-name repos, and it is materially weaker than Public’s governed scope model.
- **Recommendation**: reject
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 6: An ergonomic inline memory intake is valid only as a governed facade
- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts:138](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L138), [generate-context.js:85](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/memory/generate-context.js#L85), [memory-save.ts:1128](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-save.ts#L1128), [memory-save.ts:1273](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-save.ts#L1273); evidence: both
- **What it does**: Mnemosyne lets the agent store concise strings directly, while Public’s durable save path is JSON-primary, file-backed, preflighted, and governed.
- **Why it matters for us**: A `memory_remember`-style helper could be useful, but only if it produces the same governed save artifacts rather than bypassing them.
- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
- **Impact**: medium

### Finding 7: A direct `core=true` shortcut should not be added to Public’s main surface
- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts:140](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L140), [external/README.md:57](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/README.md#L57), [vector-index-store.ts:609](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts#L609), [memory-crud-update.ts:67](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-update.ts#L67); evidence: both
- **What it does**: Mnemosyne collapses “always-on” behavior into a simple `core` tag, while Public reserves that behavior for explicit constitutional-tier handling and budgeted cache/query logic.
- **Why it matters for us**: A shortcut would blur `constitutional`, `critical`, and ordinary memories and encourage over-promotion.
- **Recommendation**: reject
- **Impact**: medium

### Finding 8: Mnemosyne’s blind append and hard-delete lifecycle is the wrong primary mutation model for Public
- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts:147](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L147), [external/src/index.ts:193](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L193), [memory-crud-delete.ts:67](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-delete.ts#L67), [memory-crud-update.ts:138](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-update.ts#L138); evidence: both
- **What it does**: Mnemosyne exposes append-plus-delete behavior at the wrapper boundary, while Public wraps updates/deletes in transactions, history, ledgering, checkpointing, and hook invalidation.
- **Why it matters for us**: Public should keep its auditable mutation model and treat contradiction cleanup as structured revision, not as plain-text ID deletion.
- **Recommendation**: reject
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 9: A native OpenCode memory plugin is viable only as a delegation-only facade
- **Source**: [external/package.json:31](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/package.json#L31), [external/src/index.ts:38](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L38), [session-bootstrap.ts:330](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-bootstrap.ts#L330), [opencode-transport.ts:104](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/context/opencode-transport.ts#L104); evidence: both
- **What it does**: Mnemosyne shows that a thin OpenCode wrapper can feel good, and Public already emits OpenCode transport plans from MCP-owned state.
- **Why it matters for us**: If we build a native facade, it must delegate to MCP for save/search/recovery and keep CocoIndex/code-graph as separate retrieval planes, or it will become a second authority layer.
- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
- **Impact**: medium

### Finding 10: Optional offline backend readiness is worth prototyping, but Mnemosyne’s runtime stack should not become Public’s default
- **Source**: [external/README.md:18](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/README.md#L18), [external/src/index.ts:67](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L67), [context-server.ts:751](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts#L751), [session-bootstrap.ts:194](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-bootstrap.ts#L194); evidence: both
- **What it does**: Mnemosyne assumes a local binary plus a first-run model download and mainly reports failures through stderr or one install hint, while Public already has explicit bootstrap/readiness surfaces.
- **Why it matters for us**: If we ever support optional local memory backends, they need a real warmup/doctor/readiness flow; Public should not switch its primary path to Bun-plus-binary-plus-model-download.
- **Recommendation**: prototype later
- **Impact**: medium

## Assessment
- Executive summary: 10 consolidated decisions. `adopt now`: 2. `prototype later`: 2. `NEW FEATURE`: 2. `reject`: 4. The highest-confidence transfers are compaction-time memory action guidance and rewritten startup policy; the highest-confidence rejections are basename-derived scope, `core=true`, and Mnemosyne-style mutation semantics.
- New information ratio: 0.46
- Questions addressed: hybrid contract vs wrapper proof, compaction resilience, AGENTS/startup reuse, project/global ergonomics, scope identity stability, inline save ergonomics, constitutional pinning semantics, mutation/audit model, OpenCode facade design, federated retrieval separation, offline readiness tradeoffs
- Questions answered: Public should borrow Mnemosyne’s behavioral ergonomics, not its authority model. The safe path is `session_bootstrap`-aligned guidance plus a transport-owned compaction memory card, with any future aliases or facades compiled into existing MCP/governance surfaces.
- Validation: `bash .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec/validate.sh "/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main" --strict` returned `Summary: Errors: 0  Warnings: 0` and `RESULT: PASSED`, plus `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted` from the read-only sandbox.

## Reflection
- What worked: source-first tracing from [`external/src/index.ts`](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts) into Public’s transport, bootstrap, save, and mutation handlers made the true boundary obvious: Mnemosyne contributes behavior cues and wrapper ergonomics, not a stronger core memory substrate.
- What did not work: the external repo exposes no Go backend internals beyond README claims, the phase folder still lacks a canonical `research/research.md`, and the read-only sandbox prevented updating phase docs or saving phase memory.

## Recommended Next Focus
Design two packet-ready follow-ons before any runtime edits: one for a deduped OpenCode compaction memory action card owned by transport, and one for a governed `memory_remember` / read-only `project|global` facade that delegates entirely to existing MCP scope and save authority.

# Iteration 020: FINAL SYNTHESIS

## Focus
FINAL SYNTHESIS: I consolidated the repeated iteration themes into 10 non-overlapping decisions so every substantive Mnemosyne finding lands in one bucket: `adopt now`, `prototype later`, `NEW FEATURE`, or `reject`.

## Findings
### Finding 1: Mnemosyne is not a retrieval-engine upgrade for Public
- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts:106](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L106), [external/README.md:82](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/README.md#L82), [memory-search.ts:482](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts#L482), [memory-search.ts:809](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts#L809); evidence: both
- **What it does**: Mnemosyne’s wrapper forwards search requests to a backend whose BM25/vector/RRF internals are only README-documented, while Public already runs a visible multi-stage hybrid search pipeline.
- **Why it matters for us**: The transferable value is wrapper ergonomics, not ranking replacement, so using Mnemosyne as a reason to re-architect Public retrieval would be a category error.
- **Recommendation**: reject
- **Impact**: high

- **Impact**: medium

### Finding 4: Read-only `project` / `global` recall aliases are worth prototyping
- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts:118](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L118), [external/src/index.ts:160](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L160), [memory-search.ts:529](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts#L529), [memory-search.ts:818](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts#L818); evidence: wrapper-confirmed
- **What it does**: Mnemosyne gives simple project/global tool pairs; Public already has richer runtime scoping via `retrievalLevel` and broader fallback behavior.
- **Why it matters for us**: Thin read aliases could improve ergonomics, but only if they compile down to existing scope logic and do not invent new authority.
- **Recommendation**: prototype later
- **Impact**: medium

### Finding 5: Basename-derived project identity is too weak for Public’s memory authority
- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts:28](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L28), [external/src/index.ts:79](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L79), [external/README.md:55](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/README.md#L55), [memory-save.ts:1265](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-save.ts#L1265); evidence: both
- **What it does**: Mnemosyne normalizes the current directory basename into a collection key and auto-inits that collection on load.
- **Why it matters for us**: That is fragile across renames, sibling worktrees, and same-name repos, and it is materially weaker than Public’s governed scope model.
- **Recommendation**: reject
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 6: An ergonomic inline memory intake is valid only as a governed facade
- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts:138](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L138), [generate-context.js:85](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/memory/generate-context.js#L85), [memory-save.ts:1128](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-save.ts#L1128), [memory-save.ts:1273](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-save.ts#L1273); evidence: both
- **What it does**: Mnemosyne lets the agent store concise strings directly, while Public’s durable save path is JSON-primary, file-backed, preflighted, and governed.
- **Why it matters for us**: A `memory_remember`-style helper could be useful, but only if it produces the same governed save artifacts rather than bypassing them.
- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
- **Impact**: medium

### Finding 7: A direct `core=true` shortcut should not be added to Public’s main surface
- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts:140](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L140), [external/README.md:57](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/README.md#L57), [vector-index-store.ts:609](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts#L609), [memory-crud-update.ts:67](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-update.ts#L67); evidence: both
- **What it does**: Mnemosyne collapses “always-on” behavior into a simple `core` tag, while Public reserves that behavior for explicit constitutional-tier handling and budgeted cache/query logic.
- **Why it matters for us**: A shortcut would blur `constitutional`, `critical`, and ordinary memories and encourage over-promotion.
- **Recommendation**: reject
- **Impact**: medium

### Finding 8: Mnemosyne’s blind append and hard-delete lifecycle is the wrong primary mutation model for Public
- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts:147](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L147), [external/src/index.ts:193](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L193), [memory-crud-delete.ts:67](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-delete.ts#L67), [memory-crud-update.ts:138](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-update.ts#L138); evidence: both
- **What it does**: Mnemosyne exposes append-plus-delete behavior at the wrapper boundary, while Public wraps updates/deletes in transactions, history, ledgering, checkpointing, and hook invalidation.
- **Why it matters for us**: Public should keep its auditable mutation model and treat contradiction cleanup as structured revision, not as plain-text ID deletion.
- **Recommendation**: reject
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 9: A native OpenCode memory plugin is viable only as a delegation-only facade
- **Source**: [external/package.json:31](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/package.json#L31), [external/src/index.ts:38](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L38), [session-bootstrap.ts:330](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-bootstrap.ts#L330), [opencode-transport.ts:104](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/context/opencode-transport.ts#L104); evidence: both
- **What it does**: Mnemosyne shows that a thin OpenCode wrapper can feel good, and Public already emits OpenCode transport plans from MCP-owned state.
- **Why it matters for us**: If we build a native facade, it must delegate to MCP for save/search/recovery and keep CocoIndex/code-graph as separate retrieval planes, or it will become a second authority layer.
- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
- **Impact**: medium

### Finding 10: Optional offline backend readiness is worth prototyping, but Mnemosyne’s runtime stack should not become Public’s default
- **Source**: [external/README.md:18](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/README.md#L18), [external/src/index.ts:67](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L67), [context-server.ts:751](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts#L751), [session-bootstrap.ts:194](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-bootstrap.ts#L194); evidence: both
- **What it does**: Mnemosyne assumes a local binary plus a first-run model download and mainly reports failures through stderr or one install hint, while Public already has explicit bootstrap/readiness surfaces.
- **Why it matters for us**: If we ever support optional local memory backends, they need a real warmup/doctor/readiness flow; Public should not switch its primary path to Bun-plus-binary-plus-model-download.
- **Recommendation**: prototype later
- **Impact**: medium

 succeeded in 0ms:

/** Build the OpenCode runtime hook plan from the shared payload surfaces. */
export function buildOpenCodeTransportPlan(args: {
  bootstrapPayload?: SharedPayloadEnvelope | null;
  startupPayload?: SharedPayloadEnvelope | null;
  resumePayload?: SharedPayloadEnvelope | null;
  healthPayload?: SharedPayloadEnvelope | null;
  compactionPayload?: SharedPayloadEnvelope | null;
  specFolder?: string | null;
}): OpenCodeTransportPlan {
  const trackedPayloadKinds = [
    args.bootstrapPayload?.kind,
    args.startupPayload?.kind,
    args.resumePayload?.kind,
    args.healthPayload?.kind,
    args.compactionPayload?.kind,
  ].filter((kind): kind is SharedPayloadEnvelope['kind'] => !!kind);

  const systemPayload = args.bootstrapPayload ?? args.startupPayload ?? args.resumePayload ?? null;
  const messagePayloads = [args.resumePayload, args.healthPayload].filter(
    (payload): payload is SharedPayloadEnvelope => !!payload,
  );
  const compactionPayload = args.compactionPayload ?? args.resumePayload ?? args.bootstrapPayload ?? null;

  return {
    interfaceVersion: '1.0',
    transportOnly: true,
    retrievalPolicyOwner: 'runtime',
    event: {
      hook: 'event',
      trackedPayloadKinds,
      summary: args.specFolder
        ? `Track OpenCode routing hints for ${args.specFolder}`
        : 'Track OpenCode routing hints for the current packet/runtime state',
    },
    ...(systemPayload
      ? {
        systemTransform: {
          hook: 'experimental.chat.system.transform',
          title: 'OpenCode Startup Digest',
          payloadKind: systemPayload.kind,
          dedupeKey: `system:${systemPayload.kind}`,
          content: appendStartupSnapshotNote(renderBlockContent(
            systemPayload,
            'Inject this as the startup digest for hookless OpenCode recovery. Keep it transport-only.',
          ),
          ),
        },
      }
      : {}),
    messagesTransform: messagePayloads.map((payload, index) => ({
      hook: 'experimental.chat.messages.transform' as const,
      title: index === 0 ? 'OpenCode Retrieved Context' : 'OpenCode Operational Context',
      payloadKind: payload.kind,
      dedupeKey: `messages:${payload.kind}:${index}`,
      content: renderBlockContent(
        payload,
        'Inject this as bounded current-turn context. Do not treat it as retrieval policy.',
      ),
    })),
    ...(compactionPayload
      ? {
        compaction: {
          hook: 'experimental.session.compacting',
          title: 'OpenCode Compaction Resume Note',
          payloadKind: compactionPayload.kind,
          dedupeKey: `compaction:${compactionPayload.kind}`,
          content: renderBlockContent(
            compactionPayload,
            'Inject this as the continuity note across compaction. Keep it separate from current-turn retrieval.',
          ),
        },
      }
      : {}),
  };
}

 succeeded in 0ms:
  if (options && options.enableToolDispatchHook === false) {
    return null;
  }

  // Skip memory-aware tools to prevent recursive surfacing loops
  if (MEMORY_AWARE_TOOLS.has(toolName)) {
    return null;
  }

  const contextHint = extractContextHint(toolArgs);
  if (!contextHint) {
    return null;
  }

  // Delegate to the core surface function; token budget is explicitly
  // Enforced at the hook output boundary via estimateTokenCount().
  return autoSurfaceMemories(contextHint, TOOL_DISPATCH_TOKEN_BUDGET, 'tool-dispatch');
}

/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   7. COMPACTION HOOK (TM-05)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */

/**
 * autoSurfaceAtCompaction
 *
 * Fires at session compaction lifecycle points. Surfaces memories relevant
 * to the ongoing session context so that critical knowledge is preserved
 * across the compaction boundary.
 *
 * Skipped when:
 *   - sessionContext is empty or too short to extract signal
 *   - enableCompactionHook is false in the integration config
 *
 * Token budget: COMPACTION_TOKEN_BUDGET (4000 max)
 *
 * @param sessionContext - A textual summary of the current session state
 * @param options        - Optional integration-layer config flags
 * @returns AutoSurfaceResult or null if nothing to surface / hook disabled
 */
async function autoSurfaceAtCompaction(
  sessionContext: string,
  options?: { enableCompactionHook?: boolean }
): Promise<AutoSurfaceResult | null> {
  // Allow integration layer to disable this hook via config flag
  if (options && options.enableCompactionHook === false) {
    return null;
  }

  // Require a meaningful context string (at least 3 characters)
  if (!sessionContext || typeof sessionContext !== 'string' || sessionContext.trim().length < 3) {
    return null;
  }

  // Delegate to the core surface function; token budget is explicitly
  // Enforced at the hook output boundary via estimateTokenCount().
  return autoSurfaceMemories(sessionContext.trim(), COMPACTION_TOKEN_BUDGET, 'compaction');
}

/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   8. EXPORTS
──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */

export {
  // Constants
  MEMORY_AWARE_TOOLS,
  CONSTITUTIONAL_CACHE_TTL,
  TOOL_DISPATCH_TOKEN_BUDGET,
  COMPACTION_TOKEN_BUDGET,

  // Functions
  extractContextHint,
  getConstitutionalMemories,
  clearConstitutionalCache,
  autoSurfaceMemories,
  primeSessionIfNeeded,
  resetSessionPrimed,
  autoSurfaceAtToolDispatch,
  autoSurfaceAtCompaction,

  // T018: Session tracking for session_health tool
  recordToolCall,
  getSessionTimestamps,
  getLastActiveSessionId,
  isSessionPrimed,
  markSessionPrimed,
  getCodeGraphStatusSnapshot,
};

// T018: Export types for session-health handler
export type { PrimePackage, AutoSurfaceResult };
